affiliations.name: NSF National Center for Atmospheric Research

children.value: This notebook demonstrates how to directly access a NetCDF file on GDEX over HTTPS with fsspec and run analysis on it, without downloading the file to disk.
Load it with xarray and run analysis

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import fsspec
import xarray as xr
Step 1 — Find a File’s HTTPS URL

children.label: Step 1 — Find a File’s HTTPS URL

children.value: From the GDEX web portal, we can identify the direct HTTPS link to the NetCDF file:
Opening this URL in a browser typically starts a file download.
In this workflow, we instead use the URL programmatically so the data can be accessed directly in memory (via
) without first saving a local copy.
The direct file URL can be copied from the GDEX data access page.
Step 2 — Open the File Lazily with fsspec

children.label: Step 2 — Open the File Lazily with fsspec

children.value: ’s HTTP filesystem opens a remote file as a Python file-like object backed by HTTP range requests, so only the bytes you access are transferred.

# open the remote NetCDF file
file_url = 'https://data.gdex.ucar.edu/d651056/CESM2-LE/atm/proc/tseries/month_1/LHFLX/b.e21.BHISTcmip6.f09_g17.LE2-1001.001.cam.h0.LHFLX.185001-185912.nc'

fs = fsspec.filesystem("http")
remote_file = fs.open(file_url)
ds = xr.open_dataset(remote_file)

children.label: Step 3 — Load with xarray and Plot

children.value: on a variable to fetch its data into memory, then visualize it with matplotlib.

co2 = ds["co2vmr"]
# convert the cftime index to a datetime index for plotting
time_dt = ds.indexes["time"].to_datetimeindex()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.plot(time_dt, co2.values, linewidth=1.8)
ax.set_title("CO2 Volume Mixing Ratio Time Series")
ax.set_xlabel("Time")
ax.set_ylabel("co2vmr")
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.tight_layout()
fsspec combined with xarray lets you open a remote NetCDF file directly over HTTPS and read only the data you need, without storing the file locally.
